meaning: adj.有木栅的,隔绝的,被禁止的
examples: The Haitians are eligible to pursue political asylum in the US but have been barred from entry because most have the AIDS virus.
We found our way barred by rocks.
All the doors and windows were barred.
Three-term Mayor Michael Bloomberg, an independent, is barred from running again because of term limits.
One regular run, the "Executive Flight" from New York to Chicago, actually barred female passengers.
